enrichment.summary: Kyle records a casual daily vlog from Boulder on December 7th, 2016, reflecting on Pearl Harbor Day, his recent activities including visiting his mom, struggling with CD software, watching Marco Polo and other Netflix content, and continuing his ongoing project of mapping ancient Rome in Google Earth.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Sees smoking weed on Pearl Harbor Day as an expression of American freedom; believes the US and Netherlands handle legalization differently than people assume
Impressed by Netflix originals as a new generation of high-quality TV production with better cinematography than previous era shows
Wants to eventually write critiques of historical shows and documentaries; values them for learning history alongside entertainment
Committed to finishing the tracing phase before burnout sets in, hoping to reach the more exciting 3D analysis phase

claims.subject: Pearl Harbor
claims.claim: Pearl Harbor is the day America got attacked by the Japanese.

claims.subject: Patriotism
claims.claim: Expressing your American freedom by smoking weed on Pearl Harbor day is appropriate.

claims.subject: America
claims.claim: America is the only country in the world where weed is legal.

claims.subject: President
claims.claim: The president overrode the people to make weed legal in America.

claims.subject: Netherlands
claims.claim: Weed is not even officially legal in the Netherlands.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle bought something to put dry over something and wanted to make CDs but couldn't figure out how.

claims.claim: Kyle has an appointment at the Apple store to get help with making CDs.

claims.subject: Kyle's van
claims.claim: Kyle needs to take the battery out of his van before it dies.

claims.subject: Kyle's sleep schedule
claims.claim: Kyle has a half-day, half-night sleep schedule.

claims.subject: Netflix
claims.claim: Kyle's mom got him a Netflix trial and plans to pay for it after.

claims.subject: Netflix
claims.claim: Netflix is the new generation of TV.

claims.subject: Supergirl
claims.claim: Kyle had Supergirl playing in the background while working on his computer.

claims.subject: Byzantium
claims.claim: Kyle was listening to Byzantium while Supergirl was playing.

claims.subject: Marco Polo
claims.claim: Marco Polo on Netflix has as much nudity and sex as Spartacus on Stars.

claims.subject: Marco Polo
claims.claim: Marco Polo shows the panoramas of areas better than previous generations of movies.

claims.subject: Mongol court
claims.claim: The inner workings of the Mongol court and Chinese court are shown in Marco Polo.

claims.subject: History
claims.claim: Kyle wants to study the history of the Mongol and Chinese courts as he watches Marco Polo.

claims.subject: Historical shows
claims.claim: Kyle wants to critique all the historical shows and documentaries he's seen.

claims.subject: Google Earth
claims.claim: Kyle is working on building Rome on Google Earth by tracing and three-dimensionalizing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle feels like he's going over the hump with his Google Earth project but it's hard to tell because it's so big.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to work on his Google Earth project for four full days.

transcript.text: Hey, how's it going? So this is V-Log, living in Boulder, V-Log, for Wednesday, December 7th.
This is the day of Pearl Harbor, the day that America got attacked by those dirty Japanese.
So, I don't know. I guess, patriotism.
Um, so, just a second.
Probably be an appropriate thing to do to smoke a bunch of weed on the day of Pearl Harbor, because it's expressing your American freedom.
Don't forget, America's the only country in the world that has weed legal.
I think Uruguay has it legal.
That's because the president overrode the people.
People didn't want it to be legal.
And it's legal in, it's not even legal in the Netherlands, officially.
That's what I heard.
So, um, did New Zealand legalize? I can't remember.
Anyway.
So, yeah, what did I do yesterday?
Um, oh, you're right.
Yesterday was the day where I was driving around and I bought, um, I bought this thing.
The SuperDrive or whatever.
And then I wanted to make CDs so that I could play on my computer.
But, um, um, I can't figure out how to do it.
None of the programs work.
So, I have an appointment at Apple Store tomorrow.
And I'm going to have somebody there help me figure it out.
So, um, and so I went to my mom's house too.
Hung out with her, had some dinner, and got some CDs from my roommate's car.
We had the same CD in there for, like, two years.
It was driving me completely crazy.
Um, yeah, so now we have, like, two huge CD stack cases in there.
CDs.
So, yeah, snowed yesterday.
It was all slippery.
It's cool to drive around cozy, but it's kind of freaky because you slip around.
Um, I need to take the battery out of my van before it dies.
Because I don't think I'm going to drive that around anytime soon.
I'll probably do it now.
If I was warm, is this going to get all day?
It's 1.54 now.
I was working until, like, 4.30.
And then I went to bed.
I wasn't tired, but I was just, I guess, tired of working.
And, uh, I had trouble getting up this morning because it was so cold.
I should have gotten up earlier because I didn't get up until, like, noon.
Um, so I guess I have, like, half and half, um, sleep schedule.
Half day, half night.
I'm half nocturnal, half diurnal.
Um, I was listening to, I don't think I listened to the history of Byzantium yesterday because, um, my mom got me on Netflix.
I'm on the trial Netflix, but she says she's going to pay for Netflix after that.
It's only 11 bucks a month, 12 bucks a month.
And you just get so many.
It's the new generation of TV.
I didn't even know.
I mean, I saw, I saw when I, when my mom was getting her knee surgery a couple months ago, I went over there and I was watching, um, Vikings.
No, no, no, no.
Uh, Black Sails on her Comcast.
And, uh, that was cool.
But I never actually, like, you know, checked it out, what was going on.
So now I have it in my room.
And there's just so much.
I saw Supergirl.
I didn't see Supergirl, but I had it going in the background as I was working on my computer with the sound off.
And I was listening to Byzantium, but then I saw, um, but then I saw the Rome documentary on Netflix originals.
And now I'm doing Netflix original Marco Polo, which is to got just as much nudity and sex as, uh, Spartacus on stars, the new, new generation of moviemaking.
And it's really good, well done because they show the panoramas of the areas much better than they did in the previous generation, you know, like even something like HBO Rome.
Um, and, um, yeah, and all the inner workings of the court, of the Mongol court and the Chinese court.
It's pretty cool, man.
I should, uh, study the history of that as I watch the show.
I was doing that with the Rome documentary.
I want to, I want to do, uh, critiques of all the different, you know, historical shows and documentaries that I've ever seen whenever I get around to it.
Um, if you're a historian, you should probably watch the historical stuff just to see on top of how, how accurate they are.
Um, so, yeah, I'm still hammering away on my Google Earth.
Um, the idea is that I work on, I, I, uh, there's two sections to building Google Earth on Rome, or building Rome on Google Earth.
There's tracing, and then there's three-dimensionalizing.
And so, if I can get done with tracing before I burn out, then I can get into the three-dimensionalizing, which will refresh my excitement for it, and I'll be able to push through, so.
Um, but I, I do feel like I'm going over the hump, but it's really hard to tell, because it's so damn big.
So, maybe through the weekend, okay?
Today's Thursday.
Friday, Saturday, Sunday.
Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.
Because I'll probably work next week if the, if the weather gets warmer.
So, I got four full days.
So, let's see if I can finish tracing Rome in four full days.
Alright, peace out.
